query: What are the steps for the lockout tagout energy control procedure?
expected_answer: Six steps: prepare, shut down, isolate, apply lockout/tagout device, release stored energy, verify de-energization

query: What training must authorized employees receive for lockout tagout?
expected_answer: Training on hazardous energy sources, type and magnitude of energy, methods for isolation and control

query: When must lockout tagout annual inspections be performed?
expected_answer: At least annually, inspected by an authorized employee other than the one using the procedure

query: What is the difference between lockout and tagout?
expected_answer: Lockout uses a locking mechanism to prevent energization; tagout uses a warning tag when equipment cannot be locked out

query: Who qualifies as an authorized employee under OSHA 1910.147?
expected_answer: A person who locks out or tags out machines or equipment in order to perform servicing or maintenance

query: What must the energy control procedure documentation include?
expected_answer: Steps for shutting down, isolating, blocking and securing machines; steps for placement, removal and transfer of lockout/tagout devices

query: When can tagout be used instead of lockout?
expected_answer: Only when the energy isolating device is not capable of being locked out; employer must demonstrate equivalent protection

query: What must workers verify after applying lockout tagout devices?
expected_answer: Verify that the machine or equipment is isolated and de-energized by operating the normal stopping device or checking for absence of voltage

query: What must be done with stored energy after lockout tagout is applied?
expected_answer: All residual or stored energy must be relieved, disconnected, restrained or otherwise rendered safe

query: What are the re-energization steps after lockout tagout maintenance?
expected_answer: Inspect work area, ensure all employees are clear, remove lockout devices, notify affected employees, restore energy

query: What must employees do when they see equipment with a lockout device applied?
expected_answer: Do not attempt to restart or re-energize the equipment; do not remove the lockout device

query: What are OSHA requirements for electrical equipment in wet or damp locations?
expected_answer: Electrical equipment must be approved for the wet or damp location and installed to prevent moisture accumulation

query: What are the clearance requirements for working near energized electrical parts?
expected_answer: Sufficient access and working space must be provided and maintained around electrical equipment to permit safe operation and maintenance

query: What does OSHA 1910.303 require for guarding of live electrical parts?
expected_answer: Electrical parts likely to be examined, adjusted or serviced must be guarded to prevent accidental contact

query: What OSHA standard covers control of hazardous energy during maintenance?
expected_answer: 29 CFR 1910.147 covers the control of hazardous energy (lockout/tagout) for general industry

query: What documentation is required for an energy control program?
expected_answer: Written energy control procedures must be developed, documented and used for each machine or equipment with unexpected energization hazards

query: How often must employers audit their lockout tagout program?
expected_answer: Employers must certify that a periodic inspection of the energy control procedure has been performed at least annually

query: What are the responsibilities of affected employees under lockout tagout?
expected_answer: Affected employees must be instructed not to restart or re-energize equipment that is locked or tagged out

query: What must employers provide before employees perform lockout tagout?
expected_answer: Training to ensure the purpose and function of the energy control program are understood and employees have required knowledge and skills

query: What must be included in lockout tagout certification records?
expected_answer: Date of inspection, equipment inspected, names of employees included, name of authorized employee performing the inspection

query: What is the airflow rating for the Carrier 48LC rooftop unit?
expected_answer: Nominal airflow specifications per ton of capacity as listed in the Carrier 48LC product data

query: What refrigerant does the Carrier 48LC rooftop unit use?
expected_answer: R-410A refrigerant per Carrier 48LC product specifications

query: What are the electrical supply requirements for the Carrier 48LC?
expected_answer: Voltage, ampacity, and circuit breaker sizing specifications from the Carrier 48LC manual

query: What are the annual maintenance items for the Carrier 48LC rooftop unit?
expected_answer: Filter inspection, coil cleaning, motor lubrication, belt tension check, refrigerant check, drain pan inspection

query: What filter type and size does the Carrier 48LC use?
expected_answer: Filter dimensions and type (throwaway or cleanable) as specified in the Carrier 48LC manual

query: What installation clearances are required for the Carrier 48LC rooftop unit?
expected_answer: Minimum clearances for service access and airflow as specified in the Carrier 48LC installation guide

query: What are the operating weight and dimensions of the Carrier 48LC?
expected_answer: Unit weight and dimensions as listed in the Carrier 48LC product data

query: What are the cooling capacity specifications for the Carrier 48LC?
expected_answer: Nominal and rated cooling capacity in tons/BTU as listed in the Carrier 48LC product data

query: What are the installation requirements for the Trane XR15 heat pump?
expected_answer: Installation clearances, refrigerant line sizing, and electrical connection requirements from Trane XR15 product data

query: What refrigerant does the Trane XR15 heat pump use?
expected_answer: R-410A refrigerant as specified in Trane XR15 product data

query: What is the heating efficiency rating of the Trane XR15?
expected_answer: HSPF rating for the Trane XR15 split system heat pump from product data specifications

query: What are the electrical requirements for the Trane XR15?
expected_answer: Voltage, ampacity and minimum circuit ampacity listed in Trane XR15 product data

query: What is the SEER rating for the Trane XR15?
expected_answer: SEER cooling efficiency rating for the Trane XR15 as listed in product specifications

query: What are the service access requirements for the Lennox SL280 furnace?
expected_answer: Clearance and access requirements specified in the Lennox SL280 installation instructions

query: What is the gas pressure specification for the Lennox SL280 furnace?
expected_answer: Manifold gas pressure of 3.2-3.8 in WC as specified in the Lennox SL280 installation instructions

query: What are the venting requirements for the Lennox SL280 furnace?
expected_answer: Venting material, diameter, and configuration requirements from Lennox SL280 installation instructions

query: What electrical supply is required for the Lennox SL280?
expected_answer: Voltage and ampacity requirements for the Lennox SL280 as specified in the installation guide

query: What are the airflow requirements for the Lennox SL280 furnace?
expected_answer: Static pressure and CFM requirements for proper operation of the Lennox SL280 gas furnace

query: What electrical safety hazards must be guarded against per OSHA 1910.303?
expected_answer: Shock, arc flash, and electrocution hazards; live parts must be guarded against accidental contact

query: What are the OSHA requirements for electrical panel working space?
expected_answer: Clear working space required in front of electrical panels per OSHA 1910.303 to permit safe operation

query: What safety controls must be in place before electrical maintenance begins?
expected_answer: Lockout/tagout applied, energy verification completed, appropriate PPE worn per OSHA 1910.147

query: What are OSHA requirements for training workers on electrical hazards?
expected_answer: Workers must be trained on electrical hazards and safe work practices applicable to their jobs per 1910.303

query: What electrical hazards does OSHA 1910.303 cover for maintenance workers?
expected_answer: General electrical safety requirements including installation, guarding, grounding and use of electrical equipment

query: What is the purpose of periodic inspections under OSHA 1910.147?
expected_answer: To certify that energy control procedures are being followed and employees have been properly trained

query: What preventive maintenance tasks are typical for HVAC rooftop units?
expected_answer: Filter replacement, coil cleaning, refrigerant pressure check, belt tension check, lubrication, drain pan inspection

query: What are common refrigerant-related checks during HVAC preventive maintenance?
expected_answer: Suction and discharge pressure checks, superheat/subcooling measurement, leak inspection at service ports

query: What compliance steps are documented for HVAC lockout tagout during maintenance?
expected_answer: Electrical disconnection, application of lockout devices, verification of de-energization before servicing

query: What parts are commonly replaced during HVAC furnace preventive maintenance?
expected_answer: Filters, flame sensors, ignitors, belts, capacitors as needed per inspection findings

query: What fault codes or anomalies are commonly flagged on HVAC equipment during service?
expected_answer: High pressure faults, low suction pressure, failed capacitors, dirty filters, refrigerant leaks

query: What are the general electrical installation requirements per OSHA 1910.303?
expected_answer: Equipment must be approved, installed according to manufacturer instructions, in a manner that avoids hazards
